title: gynopleura rugosa
intro: Gynopleura rugosa, also known as Sida acuta, is a plant species within the Malvaceae family. It is a type of flowering plant native to tropical Africa, commonly used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties. The plant contains various compounds with anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which contribute to its health benefits, including wound healing, fever reduction, and anti-cancer properties. Gynopleura rugosa can be prepared in various herbal forms, such as decoctions, infusions, and ointments, for its medicinal use.
